mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP combined with steady month-over-month employment gains reduces recession risk premiums, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with concurrent GDP and employment growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate for future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when the pace of tightening exceeds 0.25 percentage points per quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral when the funds rate accelerates above the 0.25pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate signal a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts paired with expanding consumer credit balances indicate strengthening aggregate demand, which lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The positive return prediction reverses to negative when CPIAUCSL annualized inflation exceeds 3.0% during the housing and credit expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy business investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no positive relationship with industrial production growth over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market peak and potential economic slowdown, causing equity risk premiums to spike and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not decline significantly when the unemployment rate jumps by more than 0.5 percentage points within two months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation compress real yields and trigger central bank hawkishness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with robust housing construction activity signals broad-based economic expansion, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically when credit conditions remain accommodative.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% during periods of strong GDP and housing growth.
